UA-43.name: Avtonomna Respublika Krym
UA-43.note: Listed since Ukraine's independence (1991). Never removed.

RU-CR.name: Respublika Krym
RU-CR.note: Self-reported by Russia after 2014 annexation. ISO 3166/MA has not formally endorsed.

iso_3166.finding: ISO 3166-2 contains simultaneous entries for Crimea under both Ukraine (UA-43) and Russia (RU-CR). This dual listing is the technical root cause of platform inconsistency: systems that consume ISO data inherit the ambiguity. The ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency has not removed either entry.

key_legislation.title: Council Regulation (EU) No 692/2014 concerning restrictions on the import of goods originating in Crimea or Sevastopol
Council Decision 2014/386/CFSP concerning restrictions on goods originating in Crimea or Sevastopol
Council Decision 2014/145/CFSP concerning restrictive measures in respect of actions undermining or threatening the territorial integrity, sovereignty and independence of Ukraine
Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 — Digital Services Act
key_legislation.crimea_classification: Art 34: VLOPs must assess systemic risks including threats to civic discourse
key_legislation.relevance: Maps showing Crimea as Russia = potential systemic risk under Art 34

eurlex.finding: The EU has adopted 50+ legal acts since 2014 that explicitly classify Crimea as illegally annexed Ukrainian territory. Council Regulation 692/2014 prohibits import of goods 'originating in Crimea or Sevastopol.' Yet platforms operating under EU jurisdiction (Google, Apple, Mapbox) show Crimea as 'disputed' or 'Russian' depending on worldview settings.

ofac.description: Executive Order 13685 — Blocking Property of Certain Persons and Prohibiting Certain Transactions With Respect to the Crimea Region of Ukraine
ofac.note: OFAC explicitly calls it 'the Crimea Region of UKRAINE' in the program name
ofac.key_finding: The US sanctions program title itself says 'Crimea Region of Ukraine'

regulation_gap.legal_position: Unambiguous — Crimea is Ukrainian territory under Russian occupation (UN GA 68/262, EU Reg 692/2014, OFAC EO13685, ECHR Grand Chamber 2021)
regulation_gap.technical_standard: Ambiguous — ISO 3166-2 has dual listings (UA-43 AND RU-CR)
regulation_gap.platform_behavior: Inconsistent — Google/Bing show 'disputed', Yandex/2GIS show Russia, Nominatim shows Ukraine
regulation_gap.gap: No mechanism exists to enforce legal position in technical standards or platform geodata
